---
title: "RADseq raw data processing"
author: "Maegwin Bonar"
date: "2026-06-17"
output:
  html_document:
    highlight: breezedark
    css:
      - "styles.css"
    toc: yes
    toc_float: no
    toc_depth: 5
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 120
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  eval                        = FALSE,
  echo                        = TRUE,
  cache                       = TRUE, # tidy = TRUE,
  class.output                = "bg-success"
)

```

### Introduction

We generated restriction site-associated DNA sequencing (RADseq) libraries using an adapted protocol from Parchman et al. (2012) and Peterson et al. (2012). The libraries were sequenced at The Centre for Applied Genomics (TCAG) in The Hospital for Sick Children (SickKids), Toronto, Canada on an Illumina HiSeq 2500 to produce 2 × 126 base pair paired-end reads.

### Index reference genome

```{bash}
####### index reference genome -------------------------------------

#input: reference genome
#output: indexed reference genome

# load modules
module load bwa

# create symbolic link to reference folder
#ln -s /home/mpmb38/projects/rrg-shaferab/MAMMAL_GENOMES/odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa .

################### bwa index --------------------------------------
bwa index -a bwtsw odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa

#END
```

### Demultiplex using process_radtags

```{bash}
####### 01 demulitplex using process_radtags -------------------------------------

#input: fastq.gz files R1 and R2 ${1}_L003_R1_001.fastq.gz ${1}_L003_R2_001.fastq.gz
#output: four files per barcode, two for the single-end read and two for the paired-end read (eg. sample_ACTCG.1.fq, sample_ACTCG.rem.1.fq, sample_ACTCG.2.fq, sample_ACTCG.rem.2.fq)

################### process_radtag --------------------------------------
# load modules
module load stacks

# create symbolic link to fastq files
#ln -s /home/mpmb38/projects/rrg-shaferab/mpmb38/radseq/SRA_RADseq/*gz .

for f in $(ls Pool3*.gz.md5 | sed 's/Pool3_*.fastq.gz.md5//' | cut -f1-5 -d'_'| uniq)
do echo ${f}
mkdir ${f}_samples
sleep 3
done

# loop across all filesls b
for f in $(ls Pool3*.gz.md5 | sed 's/Pool3_*.fastq.gz//' | cut -f1-4 -d'_'| uniq)
do echo ${f}

process_radtags -1 $f\_L003_R1_001.fastq.gz -2 $f\_L003_R2_001.fastq.gz -o ${f}_samples -b barcodes/${f}_barcodes.txt -e SbfI -E phred33 -r -c -q -i gzfastq

sleep 3
done

### Make sample directory for Pool2 files
for f in $(ls Pool2*.gz.md5 | sed 's/Pool2_*.fastq.gz.md5//' | cut -f1-5 -d'_'| uniq)
do echo ${f}
mkdir ${f}_samples
sleep 3
done

```

Fastqc the files

```{bash}

######## fastqc of all files -------------------------------------
# load module
module load fastqc

for f in $(ls Pool2*.gz | sed 's/Pool2_*.fastq.gz//' | cut -f1-5 -d'_'| uniq)
do echo ${f}

fastqc ${f}_ls samples/*fq.gz -o fastqc_output

sleep 3
done


######## multiqc of all fastqc.zip files -------------------------------------
#module use /cvmfs/soft.mugqic/CentOS6/modulefiles
#module load mugqic/MultiQC/1.12
#multiqc --interactive .
```

### Adapter removal

```{bash}
####### 02 trim and adapter removal -------------------------------------

# load modules
module load trimmomatic/0.39

# make variable for output
outputdir_Q=trimmed_samples

# loop across all files
for f in $(ls *.fq.gz | cut -f1 -d'.'| uniq)
do

echo ${f}

# trim on the paired reads
java -jar $EBROOTTRIMMOMATIC/trimmomatic-0.39.jar PE -threads 32 ${f}.1.fq.gz \
${f}.2.fq.gz $outputdir_Q/${f}_paired.1.fq.gz $outputdir_Q/${f}_unpaired.1.fq.gz \
$outputdir_Q/${f}_paired.2.fq.gz $outputdir_Q/${f}_unpaired.2.fq.gz \
ILLUMINACLIP:$EBROOTTRIMMOMATIC/adapters/TruSeq3-PE.fa:2:30:10 SLIDINGWINDOW:4:10 MINLEN:100

done

```

Fastqc and check adapters were removed

```{bash}
###### 02b fastqc again to check adapter removal -------------------------------------

# make output directory for fastqc and multiqc
# mkdir fastqc_output_trim

######## fastqc of all files -------------------------------------
# load module
module load fastqc

fastqc *fq.gz -o fastqc_output_trim

######## multiqc of all fastqc.zip files -------------------------------------

#module load mugqic/MultiQC/1.12
#multiqc --interactive .
```

### Map, samtobam, sort, index samples to reference genome

Paired reads

```{bash}
####### 03a map, samtobam, sort, index samples to reference genome -------------------------------------

#input: paired  reads with adapters removed 1.fq.gz and 2.fq.gz
#output: mapped, sorted, indexed bam file for paired reads

##### load modules
module load bwa samtools

##### create symbolic link to reference folder
#ln -s /home/mpmb38/projects/rrg-shaferab/MAMMAL_GENOMES/odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa .

##### make directories for sam and bam files
# mkdir sam_files
# mkdir bam_files

### set variables
ref=/home/mpmb38/scratch

##### call the samples with the for loop
for f in $(ls *.fq.gz | cut -f1 -d'.'| uniq)
do

echo ${f}

	################### bwa index --------------------------------------
	
	bwa mem -t 32 $ref\/odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa trimmed_samples/${f}_paired.1.fq.gz trimmed_samples/${f}_paired.2.fq.gz > sam_files/${f}_paired.sam

	################### sam to bam files --------------------------------------

	samtools view -S -b -@ 32 sam_files/${f}_paired.sam > bam_files/${f}_paired.bam
	
	################### sort the bam file --------------------------------------

	samtools sort -@ 32 bam_files/${f}_paired.bam -o bam_files/${f}_sorted_paired.bam
	
	################### index the bam file --------------------------------------

	samtools index bam_files/${f}_sorted_paired.bam
	
done
#END

```

Unpaired reads

```{bash}
####### 03b map, samtobam, sort, index unpaired samples to reference genome -------------------------------------

#input: unpaired reads with adapters removed 1.fq.gz and 2.fq.gz
#output: mapped, sorted, indexed bam file for unpaired reads

##### load modules
module load bwa samtools

##### create symbolic link to reference folder
#ln -s /home/mpmb38/projects/rrg-shaferab/MAMMAL_GENOMES/odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa .

##### make directories for sam and bam files
# mkdir sam_files
# mkdir bam_files

### set variables
ref=/home/mpmb38/scratch

##### call the samples with the for loop
for f in $(ls *.fq.gz | cut -f1 -d'.'| uniq)
do

echo ${f}

	################### bwa index --------------------------------------
	
	bwa mem -t 32 $ref\/odocoileus_virginianus_NEW.fa trimmed_samples/${f}_unpaired.1.fq.gz trimmed_samples/${f}_unpaired.2.fq.gz > sam_files/${f}_unpaired.sam

	################### sam to bam files --------------------------------------

	samtools view -S -b -@ 32 sam_files/${f}_unpaired.sam > bam_files/${f}_unpaired.bam
	
	################### sort the bam file --------------------------------------

	samtools sort -@ 32 bam_files/${f}_unpaired.bam -o bam_files/${f}_sorted_unpaired.bam
	
	################### index the bam file --------------------------------------

	samtools index bam_files/${f}_sorted_unpaired.bam
	
done
#END

```

### Merge paired and unpaired bam files

```{bash}
####### 04 merge paired and unpaired bam files then index -------------------------------------

#input: paired and unparied mapped, sorted, indexed bam files
#output: merged and indexed final bam files

##### load modules
module load bwa samtools

##### call the samples with the for loop
for f in $(ls *.fq.gz | cut -f1 -d'.'| uniq)
do

echo ${f}
	

	################### merge paired and unpaired bam files --------------------------------------
	
	samtools merge bam_files/${f}_final.bam bam_files/${f}_sorted_paired.bam bam_files/${f}_sorted_unpaired.bam

	################### index merged bam files --------------------------------------

	samtools index bam_files/${f}_final.bam
	
done
#END

```

QC

```{bash}
################### Make nice output from flagstat and coverage and depth qc ---------------------------------

##### coverage
# Call the ids 
for i in $(ls *.tsv | sed 's/.tsv//')
do
 echo $i
 # Print scaffold, covered bases, depth, base quality, mapping quality, sample name
awk -v OFS='\t' 'NR!=1 {print $1, $5, $7, $8, $9 "\t'$i'"}' $i.tsv >$i.tmp
done

# paste the scaffolds file and all the covered base files together
cat *tmp > covered_bases.txt

# remove the temp files
rm -rf *tmp

##### depth
# Call the ids 
for i in $(ls *.txt | sed 's/.txt//')
do
 echo $i
 # Print whole depth and sample name
awk '{print $1 "\t'$i'"}' $i.txt >$i.tmp
done

# cat the files together
cat *tmp > whole_depths_tmp.txt

# now transpose it by sample id
awk '{ rec[$2]=rec[$2] " " $1 }
END { for (key in rec) print key rec[key] }
' whole_depths_tmp.txt | sort > whole_depths.txt

# remove the temp files
rm -rf *tmp

##### flagstat
# Call the ids 
for i in $(ls *.tsv | sed 's/.tsv//')
do
 echo $i
 # Print whole depth and sample name
awk '{print $1 "\t'$i'"}' $i.tsv >$i.tmp
done

# cat the files together
cat *tmp > statstmp.txt

# now transpose it by sample id
awk '{ rec[$2]=rec[$2] " " $1 }
END { for (key in rec) print key rec[key] }
' statstmp.txt | sort > stats.txt

# remove the temp files
rm -rf *tmp

```


### Stacks

```{bash}
####### 05 use gstacks which will read in aligned reads to assemble loci -------------------------------------

#input: bam files and a population map which lists all samples with their population
#output: aligned and genotyped bam files

##### load modules
module load nixpkgs/16.09  gcc/7.3.0
module load stacks/2.3e

##### run gstacks, call final bams and population map
gstacks -I ./final_bams/ -M ./popmap/population.map2.txt -O ./stacks/ -t 32

#END
```

### Populations

```{bash}
####### 06 use populations to get vcf files -------------------------------------

#input: output of gstacks and a population map which lists all samples with their population
#output: vcf files

##### load modules
module load nixpkgs/16.09 gcc/7.3.0
module load stacks/2.3e

##### run gstacks, call final bams and population map
populations -P ./stacks/ -M ./popmap/population.map4.txt -r 0.80 --min-maf 0.01 --max-obs-het 0.8 --write-single-snp --vcf -O ./populations_map4_r0.8_indrm/ -t 32

#END

#### Remove individuals with missing snps -----------

# First make plink file out of vcf
plink --vcf populations.snps.vcf --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r08_90_indrm
plink --vcf populations.snps.vcf --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r08_90_indrm_may23

# Next recode the bed file with individuals with too much missing data removed
# 70%
plink --bfile popmap4r08 --const-fid --allow-extra-chr --mind 0.3 --recode --out cleaned_70

# 90%
plink --bfile popmap4r08_90_indrm --const-fid --allow-extra-chr --mind 0.1 --recode --out cleaned_90_indrm
plink --bfile popmap4r08_90_indrm_may23 --const-fid --allow-extra-chr --mind 0.1 --recode --out cleaned_90_indrm

# make a vcf file out of it
plink --file cleaned_90_indrm --recode vcf --allow-extra-chr --const-fid --out popmap4r08_90_indrm_may23

# 95%
plink --bfile popmap4r08 --const-fid --allow-extra-chr --mind 0.05 --recode --out cleaned_95

# 99%
plink --bfile popmap4r08 --const-fid --allow-extra-chr --mind 0.01 --recode --out cleaned_99

```

### Create GRM and filter

```{bash}
####### 07 create GRM and filter -------------------------------------

#input: vcf file from populations
#output: GRM unfiltered and with filtering applied

# Software dependancies that need to be in your PATH:cd
#	 - VCFtools version 0.1.12a
#	 - PLINK version 1.90(b3.34)
# 	 - GCTA version 1.92.4

##### Set your objects
VCF_IN=populations_r0.5/populations.snps.dp.hwe.recode.vcf
VCF_IN=populations_r0.5/populations.snps.vcf
VCF_IN=populations_map4_r0.5/populations.snps.dp.hwe.lm.recode.vcf
OUT_DIR=gcta_grm/filtered_map4_nonimpute
GRM_CUTOFF="0.05 0.1"
#INDIV_TO_RM=indiv_to_rm.list

##### Setting of quality filter thresholds
# Loci missingness filter
#MAX_MISS="0.7 0.8"
# Minor allele frequency filter
#MIN_MAF="0.01 0.05 0.10"

##### Make output directory
if [[ ! -d $OUT_DIR ]]
then
mkdir $OUT_DIR
else
    echo $OUT_DIR already exits! >&2 
fi

#if [[ ! -e $INDIV_TO_RM ]]
#then 
#echo "Your indive file list to remove does not exist"
#exit
#fi

##### Make a Genomic Relatedness Matrix for the unfiltered dataset
# Make output directory for all values
#mkdir $OUT_DIR/unfiltered
# Make an output object
#OUT_UNF=$OUT_DIR/unfiltered/unfiltered

#grep "#" $VCF_IN  > $OUT_UNF.vcf

#keep only one SNPs by loci
#grep -v "#" $VCF_IN | sort -u -k3,3 >> $OUT_UNF.vcf

# sort (needed for tabix)
#vcf-sort $OUT_UNF.vcf > ${OUT_UNF}_sort.vcf

# Get scaffold numberls rem
#bgzip ${OUT_UNF}_sort.vcf
#tabix ${OUT_UNF}_sort.vcf.gz
#zcat ${OUT_UNF}_sort.vcf.gz | grep -v "^#" | cut -f1 | sort | uniq > scaff_names
#scaff=`wc -l < scaff_names`
#echo $scaff

# Generate PLINK binary file set: bed, bim, fam
plink --file cleaned_90 --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r80_90
# Generate PLINK binary file set: bed, bim, fam
plink --file cleaned_95 --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r80_95
# Generate PLINK binary file set: bed, bim, fam
plink --file cleaned_99 --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r80_99

plink --file cleaned_90_indrm --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out cleaned_may23

plink --vcf popmap4_r80_90_indrm.recode.vcf --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out popmap4r80_indrm_90

#vcftools --vcf $OUT_UNF.vcf --bed --remove $INDIV_TO_RM --allow-extra-chr --out $OUT_UNF

# Set the bed file
OUT_UNF=cleaned_may23

# get unique scaffolds
awk '{print $1}' $OUT_UNF.bim | sort | uniq > $OUT_UNF.id
# add sequence of numbers
awk -F, '{print FNR,$0}' 'OFS=\t' $OUT_UNF.id > $OUT_UNF.id.tmp
mv $OUT_UNF.id.tmp $OUT_UNF.id
# change every number after 100 to 100
awk '{print ($1>100)? 100 : $1,$2}' $OUT_UNF.id > $OUT_UNF.id.tmp
mv $OUT_UNF.id.tmp $OUT_UNF.id


# Use the awk find and replace to replace within the .bim file
awk 'FNR==NR { array[$2]=$1; next } { for (i in array) gsub(i, array[i]) }1' $OUT_UNF.id $OUT_UNF.bim > $OUT_UNF.bim.tmp

# Work around to get rid of scaffold problem (note this was the old technique)
#awk '{$1="0";print $0}' $OUT_UNF.bim > $OUT_UNF.bim.tmp
mv $OUT_UNF.bim.tmp $OUT_UNF.bim

#awk '{$1="contig" $1;}1' $OUT_UNF.bim > $OUT_UNF.bim.tmp
#mv $OUT_UNF.bim.tmp $OUT_UNF.bim


# make the GRM without any relatedness cutoff
#gcta64 --bfile $OUT_UNF --make-grm --autosome --autosome-num 100 --out $OUT_UNF
# trying with Aidan's version of gcta 1.92.4 to see if the autosome number can be changed to be over 100
ref=/home/mpmb38/projects/rrg-shaferab/mpmb38/radseq
$ref/gcta_1.92.4beta2/gcta64 --bfile $OUT_UNF --make-grm --ibc --autosome --autosome-num 100 --out $OUT_UNF
$ref/gcta_1.92.4beta2/gcta64 --bfile $OUT_UNF --make-grm --autosome --autosome-num 100 --out $OUT_UNF

# make GRM removing highly related individuals
GRM_CUTOFF="0.05 0.1"

for cut in $GRM_CUTOFF
do
	# Make an output object
	OUT_CUTOFF=${OUT_UNF}_nr_$cut
	# echo the cutoff value to check
	echo -e "\n\n### GRM cutoff $cut"
	# using the unfiltered GRM, create new GRM with related individuals removed according to cutoff values
	$ref/gcta_1.92.4beta2/gcta64 --grm $OUT_UNF --grm-cutoff $cut --make-grm --autosome --autosome-num 100 --out $OUT_CUTOFF
done


##### Beginning of the filtering procedure 

### 1. Loci missingness filter
# Loci missingness filter is applied at the vcf file stage
for miss in $MAX_MISS
do
	# Make output directory for all values
	mkdir $OUT_DIR/lm_$miss
	
	# Make an output object
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	# echo the loci missingness 
	echo -e "\n\n### Max Miss $miss"
	
	# Apply filter to recode vcf
	vcftools --vcf $VCF_IN  --max-missing $miss --out $OUT_MISS --recode
	
	#keep only one SNPs by loci
	grep "#" $OUT_MISS.recode.vcf  > $OUT_MISS.vcf
	grep -v "#" $OUT_MISS.recode.vcf | sort -u -k3,3 >> $OUT_MISS.vcf
done 

for miss in $MAX_MISS
do
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	# Generate PLINK binary file set: bed, bim, fam
	plink --vcf $OUT_MISS.vcf --const-fid --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out $OUT_MISS
done

for miss in $MAX_MISS
do
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	# get unique scaffolds
	awk '{print $1}' $OUT_MISS.bim | sort | uniq > $OUT_MISS.id
	# add sequence of numbers
	awk -F, '{print FNR,$0}' 'OFS=\t' $OUT_MISS.id > $OUT_MISS.id.tmp
	mv $OUT_MISS.id.tmp $OUT_MISS.id
	# change every number after 100 to 100
	awk '{print ($1>100)? 100 : $1,$2}' $OUT_MISS.id > $OUT_MISS.id.tmp
	mv $OUT_MISS.id.tmp $OUT_MISS.id
done

for miss in $MAX_MISS
do	
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	# Use the awk find and replace to replace within the .bim file
	awk 'FNR==NR { array[$2]=$1; next } { for (i in array) gsub(i, array[i]) }1' $OUT_MISS.id $OUT_MISS.bim > $OUT_MISS.bim.tmp
	# Work around to get rid of scaffold problem
	#awk '{$1="0";print $0}' $OUT_MISS.bim > $OUT_MISS.bim.tmp
	mv $OUT_MISS.bim.tmp $OUT_MISS.bim
done

for miss in $MAX_MISS
do	
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	num=`tail -1 $OUT_MISS.id | awk '{print $1}'`
	# make the GRM without any relatedness cutoff
	gcta_1.92.4beta2/gcta64  --bfile $OUT_MISS --make-grm --autosome --autosome-num $num --out $OUT_MISS
done

for miss in $MAX_MISS
do	
	echo $miss
	OUT_MISS=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/lm$miss
	# make GRM removing highly related individuals
	for cut in $GRM_CUTOFF
	do
		
		# Make an output object
		OUT_CUTOFF=${OUT_MISS}_nr_$cut
		# echo the cutoff value to check
		echo -e "\n\n### GRM cutoff $cut"
		# using the unfiltered GRM, create new GRM with related individuals removed according to cutoff values
		gcta_1.92.4beta2/gcta64 --grm $OUT_MISS --grm-cutoff $cut --make-grm --autosome --autosome-num 100 --out $OUT_CUTOFF
	done
done	

### 2. Minimum Allele Frequencies filter
# Minor allele frequency filter is applied at the GRM creation stage
	for maf in $MIN_MAF
	do
		# Make output directory for all values
		mkdir $OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/maf_$maf
		# Make an output object
		OUT_MAF=$OUT_DIR/lm_$miss/maf_$maf/lm${miss}_maf$maf
		# echo the minimun allele frequency 
		echo -e "\n\n\t### Max MAF $maf"
		
		# make the GRM without any relatedness cutoff
		gcta64 --bfile $OUT_MISS --maf $maf --make-grm --out $OUT_MAF
		
		# make GRM removing highly related individuals
		for cut in $GRM_CUTOFF
		do
			# Make an output object
			OUT_CUTOFF=${OUT_MAF}_nr_$cut
			# echo the cutoff value to check
			echo -e "\n\n### GRM cutoff $cut"
			# using the unfiltered GRM, create new GRM with related individuals removed according to cutoff values
			gcta64 --grm $OUT_MAF --grm-cutoff $cut --make-grm --out $OUT_CUTOFF
		done
		
	# done MAF	
	done
# done LM
done

```
